evidence: The TrustToken route caveat is returned in ResolvedContext but is never read by aog-gateway; the concrete route decision and provider sink are directly connected.

method: static full-chain trace plus repository symbol search
evidence: confirmed: no allowed_routes enforcement exists between verified token and dispatch
proof_gap: No runtime harness was added; target code is read-only.

source: Authenticated caller uses a token restricted to local routes and selects a configured cloud-backed model with benign content.
closest_control: classify_and_route and PolicyEngine gate content sensitivity, but no code intersects the selected/effective route with ctx.token.allowed_routes.
sink_or_broken_control: Anthropic message SSE can dispatch to a cloud provider after content policy allows it even when the signed token has no CloudAllowed authority.
impact: A local-only token can cause cloud egress and provider use, defeating token attenuation and sovereign routing policy.

attack_path.attacker: holder of a valid route-restricted virtual key
preconditions: benign text receives CloudAllowed from router/policy
attack_path.closest_control_failure: content classification does not enforce token route authority
severity_facts: can violate local-first and signed attenuation invariants
